qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Concurrent policy-data subscription writes crash UDR
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different endpoint, handler, and root cause. This finding is a concurrent-map-write crash in `POST /nudr-dr/v2/policy-data/subs-to-notify` via `HandlePolicyDataSubsToNotifyPost` and `PolicyDataSubsToNotifyPostProcedure`, while the advisory is an invalid-path unauthorized delete in `DELETE /nudr-dr/v2/application-data/influenceData/{influenceId}/{subscriptionId}` caused by a missing `return` in `HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's missing-return path-validation bug on the influence-data delete handler. The only final qualifying finding is a separate policy-data concurrency denial-of-service issue.
evidence: The advisory describes `DELETE /nudr-dr/v2/application-data/influenceData/{influenceId}/{subscriptionId}` and a missing `return` after `c.String(http.StatusNotFound, "404 page not found")` in `HandleApplicationDataInfluenceDataSubsToNotifySubscriptionIdDelete`, which allows deletion to continue on an invalid `influenceId`.

The session manifest at `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_02024d9b/.judge/input_manifest.json` lists exactly one final `qualifying_vuln` finding: `policy-data-subs-to-notify-concurrent-map-write-dos`.

That finding's report and PoC artifacts target `POST /nudr-dr/v2/policy-data/subs-to-notify`, `HandlePolicyDataSubsToNotifyPost`, and `PolicyDataSubsToNotifyPostProcedure`, with runtime evidence of `fatal error: concurrent map writes`, not the advisory's influence-data delete path.

The session checklist in `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_02024d9b/artifacts/component_family_checklist.md` says the influence-data sibling path was pruned and the accepted final root cause was the policy-data concurrent-map-write crash.
